differential_diagnoses: Lymphoma (e.g., indolent B-cell or T-cell lymphoma)
next_steps: Perform immunohistochemical staining on tissue biopsy for CD68, CD1a, S100, and factor XIIIa to exclude histiocytic neoplasms
Send tissue and/or blood for BRAF V600E mutation testing
Obtain serum IgG4, total IgG, and electrophoresis with immunofixation
Review all pathology slides with a hematopathologist specializing in inflammatory infiltrates and histiocytosis
Perform a cardiac assessment (e.g., cardiac MRI or echocardiogram) as Erdheim-Chester disease frequently involves the heart
Initiate a therapeutic trial of glucocorticoids after finalizing diagnostic testing
